questions.questionText: Q1. পরমাণু সংখ্যা (atomic number) কী নির্দেশ করে? English: What does atomic number indicate?
questions.explanation: Atomic number = number of protons in nucleus.

questions.questionText: Q2. ভর সংখ্যা (mass number) = ? English: Mass number equals?
questions.explanation: Mass number A = Z + N (protons + neutrons).

questions.questionText: Q3. আইসোটপ কি? English: Isotope are atoms with?
questions.explanation: Isotopes have same atomic number but different mass numbers.

questions.questionText: Q4. Na (Z=11) এর ইলেকট্রনিক কনফিগারেশন কী? English: Electronic configuration of Na (Z=11)?
questions.explanation: 11 electrons → 2,8,1.

questions.questionText: Q5. কোন অনুপাতিকতা (trend) যখন period অনুযায়ী ডানে যাওয়া হয়? English: Across a period left→right, atomic radius generally?
questions.explanation: Increasing nuclear charge pulls electrons closer → radius decreases.

questions.questionText: Q6. Ionization energy across a period left→right সাধারণত কীভাবে থাকে? English: Ionization energy across a period left→right?
questions.explanation: Higher nuclear charge → more tightly held electrons → IE increases.

questions.questionText: Q7. Electronegativity কোনদিকে বেশি হয়? English: Electronegativity increases in which direction on periodic table?
questions.optionA: Across period to right and up the group
questions.explanation: Most EN at top-right (excluding noble gases); increases right & up.

questions.questionText: Q8. কোনটি একটি অ্যালকালাইন মেটাল? (Choose alkali metal)
questions.explanation: Potassium (K) is Group 1 alkali metal.

questions.questionText: Q9. কোনটি হ্যালোজেন? English: Which is a halogen?
questions.explanation: Chlorine (Cl) is a halogen (Group 17).

questions.questionText: Q10. Noble gases-এর প্রধান বৈশিষ্ট্য? English: Main property of noble gases?
questions.optionA: Inertness due to full valence shell
questions.explanation: Noble gases have full valence shells → low reactivity.

questions.questionText: Q11. Which species is isoelectronic with Ne (10 electrons)? English: Isoelectronic with Ne?
questions.explanation: F- (9+1), O2- (8+2), Na+ (11-1) all have 10 electrons → isoelectronic.

questions.questionText: Q12. If atomic number Z=16, element is? English: Element with Z=16 is?
questions.explanation: Atomic number 16 corresponds to sulfur.

questions.questionText: Q13. Valency of oxygen normally is? English: Typical valency of oxygen?
questions.explanation: Oxygen usually forms 2 bonds (valency 2).

questions.questionText: Q14. Which has greater metallic character: K or Cl? English: Greater metallic character between K and Cl?
questions.explanation: Metals are on left; K is metal whereas Cl is nonmetal.

questions.questionText: Q15. Electron affinity definition: it is?
questions.optionA: Energy change when an atom gains an electron
questions.explanation: Electron affinity = energy change on gaining an electron (often negative).

questions.questionText: Q16. Which element has highest electronegativity? (among H, Li, F)
questions.explanation: Fluorine is most electronegative element.

questions.questionText: Q17. Which ion forms when chlorine atom gains one electron? English: Ion formed by Cl gaining electron?
questions.explanation: Chlorine gains one electron to form Cl- (chloride).

questions.questionText: Q18. Electronic configuration 2,8,7 corresponds to which element? English: Element with config 2,8,7?
questions.explanation: 2+8+7=17 → atomic number 17 → Chlorine.

questions.questionText: Q19. Which of the following has smallest atomic radius? (Na, Mg, Al)
questions.explanation: Across period, radius decreases → Al (to the right) smallest among listed.

questions.questionText: Q20. Which element shows variable oxidation states commonly? (Fe, Na, Ne)
questions.explanation: Transition metal iron shows multiple oxidation states.

questions.questionText: Q21. What is the charge on Mg ion in MgO? English: Charge on Mg in MgO?
questions.explanation: Mg loses two electrons → Mg2+; O2- balances charge.

questions.questionText: Q22. Which element is a metalloid? (B, C, Na, Ar)
questions.explanation: Boron shows properties between metals and nonmetals → metalloid.

questions.questionText: Q23. Successive ionization energies: which atom has much higher 3rd IE indicating removal from core? (Mg: 1st~738kJ/mol, 2nd~1450kJ/mol, 3rd~7730kJ/mol) — What does a big jump indicate?
questions.explanation: A large jump indicates removal of core electron after valence removed.

questions.questionText: Q24. Which of following elements will form +1 ion most readily? (K, Ca, Al)
questions.explanation: Alkali metal K loses one electron → K+.

questions.questionText: Q25. Flame test: sodium gives what color? English: Sodium flame test color?
questions.explanation: Sodium ions produce intense yellow flame.
